channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Amid cyberattack, nation's largest water utility pauses billing
item.description: American Water provides services to more than 14 million people across 14 states and on 18 military installations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led challenge to Biden effort to ease voter registration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday turned away a challenge from Republican state lawmakers in Pennsylvania to a Biden administration executive order that is intended to boost voter registration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton strengthens into a Category 5 as Florida prepares for evacuations
item.description: Milton rapidly strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, becoming a Category 5 storm on a path toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors in Nevada quietly dismiss Chasing Horse's long-dormant sex abuse case
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Federal prosecutors in Nevada dismissed a long-dormant sex abuse case against Nathan Chasing Horse. The dismissal of the federal case was granted Oct. 1.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un warned again that he could use nuclear weapons in potential conflicts with South Korea and the United States, as he accused them of provoking North Korea and raising animosities on the Korean Peninsula, state media reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vigils, protests around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico has been killed just one week after he took office, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A peek inside human brain shows a way it cleans out waste
item.description: A unique peek inside the human brain may help explain how it clears away waste like the kind that leads to Alzheimer's disease.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: As Milton takes aim at Florida, why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: The last time the eye of a major storm like Hurricane Milton struck Tampa Bay, in 1921, the city was a sleepy backwater of a few hundred thousand people.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scott Peterson given discovery rights by California judge, 20 years after murder conviction
item.description: A judge granted Scott Peterson, who was convicted of the 2002 murder of his wife Laci and their unborn son, rights to a discovery period. The move opens the possibility of a retrial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: If you bought this ice cream, you could be eligible for a payout
item.description: If you've enjoyed vanilla-flavored Breyers ice cream over the last eight years, you may be in line for a sweet treat: a cash refund. As KTLA sister station WGN reports, Breyers is paying nearly $9 million to settle a class-action lawsuit filed against Conopco, Inc., and Unilever United States, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, KTLA 5

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert
item.description: All proceeds from the "Concert for Carolina," go to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, according to an announcement.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Aircrew rescues 2 hunters from remote California mountainside
item.description: According to RSO, the two individuals, who were hunting at the time, were experiencing heat-related injuries and were unable to continue their expedition.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Body of South Dakota woman found inside shark off Indonesian coast
item.description: "I thought it had swallowed plastic or a fishing net. It was cut open to find the problem and inside there were the remains of a woman," the fisherman who caught the shark said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doorn, NewsNation

item.title: Salmon swim freely in the Klamath River for 1st time in a century after dams removed
item.description: HORNBROOK, Calif. (AP) — For the first time in more than a century, salmon are swimming freely along the Klamath River and its tributaries — a major watershed near the California-Oregon border — just days after the largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Feds might use Greensboro immigrant facility to house displaced Helene survivors, Rep. Kathy Manning says
item.description: "The federal government has a contract to use that influx care center," Rep. Kathy Manning said. "It was a former boarding school.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Al Pacino claims he almost died of COVID: 'I was gone'
item.description: During a podcast appearance on The New York Times’ “The Interview," Al Pacino claims he once nearly died of COVID.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado black bear attacks 74-year-old man after breaking into home, leaving 'significant' claw wounds
item.description: A 74-year-old Colorado man has suffered "significant" claw injuries after being attacked by a black bear who entered his home in Lake City, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: "This is not a situation to capitalize on for political gain," the editorial board of the Charlotte Observer wrote this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rules the FTC can proceed with antitrust lawsuit against Amazon, tosses out few state claims
item.description: A federal judge said the Federal Trade Commission can proceed with its landmark antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. But, he also gave the company a small victory by tossing out a few claims made by states involved in the legal fight. The order, issued last week by Judge John H.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson sidesteps question on whether Trump lost 2020 election: ‘This is a gotcha game’
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday sidestepped a question about whether former President Trump lost the 2020 presidential election, calling it a “gotcha game” that he would not play.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Missing woman found dead in suitcase at Seattle homeless encampment
item.description: Police confirmed that a body found in a suitcase in a Seattle homeless encampment was 37-year-old Shannon Marie Caslin Reeder on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of Gaza and southern Lebanon ahead of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: A new round of airstrikes hit Beirut suburbs late Sunday as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region. Palestinian officials said a strike on a mosque killed at least 19 people.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina is distributing Benadryl and EpiPens as yellow jackets swarm from Helene flooding
item.description: Deadly flooding from Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina has also disrupted the underground nests of yellow jackets, bees and other insects, causing them to swarm and sting people struggling to recover from the storm.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee state authorities said Wednesday they were investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4
